input.background: The amount of nitrogen (in kilotonnes per annum) entering UK coastal waters via fresh water inputs (i.e. from rivers) is compared to that entering directly into saline waters from point sources (e.g. pipes).
input.question: Using multiple comparison tests where appropriate, what can you conclude from these results?

output.answer: River input is significantly higher than direct input (River=42; Direct=23.2), and there is a significant difference among regions. Tukey's multiple comparisons reveal that the North Sea East Coast has significantly higher nitrogen input than all the other regions (P<0.05). None of the other regions differ from each other: North Sea East Coast > (Irish Sea, Celtic Sea, Atlantic, North Sea Channel).

meta_info.chapter: Differences between more than two samples
meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
